content:
1:  /*
2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.
3:  */
4: 
5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule

407: ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
408: 
409: module connectivity_mapping #(
410:     parameter NB_MANAGER      = 4,
411:     parameter NB_SUBORDINATE       = 4,
412:     parameter NB_PRIV_LVL    = 4,   
413:     parameter PRIV_LVL_WIDTH = 4   
414:   )(
415:     input logic [PRIV_LVL_WIDTH-1:0] 	       priv_lvl_i,   
416:     input logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0][NB_PRIV_LVL-1:0] access_ctrl_i,  
417:     
418:     output logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0]  connectivity_map_o
419:   ); 
420: 
421:     
422:   genvar i,j ; 
423: 
424:   generate
425:     
426:     for (i=0; i<NB_SUBORDINATE; i++)
427:     begin
428:       	for (j=0; j<NB_MANAGER; j++)
429:       	begin
430: 	  assign connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]) ; 
431:       	end  		    		      		
432:     end
433: 
434:   endgenerate 
435: 
436: endmodule

{
  "scope_id": "module2_permission_obligations_current_scope",
  "results": [
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-AXI-CONN-PRIV-001",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "AXI requesters on slave[0] (Ariane core) and slave[1] (debug module AXI master) entering axi_node_intf_wrap.",
      "operation": "Route AXI read/write transactions to target/peripheral master ports.",
      "object": "Subordinate/target access policy for ariane_soc targets DRAM=0, GPIO=1, Ethernet=2, SPI=3, AES=4, UART=5, PLIC=6, CLINT=7, PKT=8, ROM2=9, ROM=10, Debug=11.",
      "expected_guard": "For requester i and target j, route permission should be access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i], unless an intentional, documented alias is present and safe.",
      "observed_behavior": "connectivity_mapping grants connectivity_map_o[i][j] when access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] is set, but also grants target j==6 when access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] is set. In ariane_soc_pkg, j==6 is PLIC and j==7 is CLINT, so CLINT permission also grants PLIC connectivity.",
      "path": "tb/ariane_testharness.sv access_ctrl_reg -> access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node_intf_wrap.access_ctrl_i -> connectivity_mapping.connectivity_map_o -> axi_node.cfg_connectivity_map_i.",
      "state_condition": "All privilege levels selected by priv_lvl_i; specific condition j==6 / PLIC and access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]==1; NB_PRIV_LVL is riscv::NB_PRIV_LVL and priv_lvl_i width is riscv::PRIV_LVL_WIDTH in the harness.",
      "impact": "A requester authorized for CLINT can be routed to PLIC even if PLIC permission is not set, potentially allowing interrupt-controller MMIO access beyond the configured policy.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430: assign connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]);",
        "tb/ariane_soc_pkg.sv:28-29: PLIC = 6, CLINT = 7.",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:450: assign access_ctrl[i][j] = access_ctrl_reg[i][4*j +: 4];",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:471-472: .priv_lvl_i(priv_lvl), .access_ctrl_i(access_ctrl).",
        "src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:383: .cfg_connectivity_map_i(s_connectivity_map)."
      ]
    },
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-AXI-RANGE-ROUTE-002",
      "status": "inconclusive",
      "subject": "AXI requesters on the wrapper slave ports.",
      "operation": "Route AW/AR transactions by address to target/peripheral ports.",
      "object": "Address-map protected regions: Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM.",
      "expected_guard": "AW/AR address must match configured start/end range and connectivity/access-control policy before target valid reaches a manager/peripheral port.",
      "observed_behavior": "The wrapper forwards slave AW/AR address/valid/prot/user into axi_node and passes configured start/end ranges and connectivity map into axi_node. The wrapper itself assigns s_valid_rule='1 and does not contain the range comparison or invalid-access response logic; that logic is inside axi_node, whose implementation is not visible in this scope.",
      "path": "slave[j].aw/ar_* -> s_slave_aw/ar_* -> axi_node_i -> s_master_aw/ar_* -> master[i].aw/ar_*.",
      "state_condition": "Address ranges are configured from ariane_soc_pkg bases and lengths. The visible ranges are non-overlapping by their listed base/length values, but the effect of s_valid_rule='1 and invalid/out-of-range responses cannot be proven without axi_node internals.",
      "impact": "If axi_node treats all rules as valid without enforcing start/end, address-based isolation could be over-broadened; if axi_node enforces cfg_START/END normally, the obligation is satisfied. Visible RTL is insufficient to decide.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:282-321: slave AW/AR channels are connected into axi_node_i.",
        "src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:331-370: master AW/AR channels are driven from axi_node_i outputs.",
        "src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:380-383: cfg_START_ADDR_i, cfg_END_ADDR_i, cfg_valid_rule_i, cfg_connectivity_map_i connected to axi_node_i.",
        "src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:387: assign s_valid_rule = '1;",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:473-500: start_addr_i/end_addr_i populated from ariane_soc base/length constants.",
        "tb/ariane_soc_pkg.sv:38-66: region lengths and bases."
      ]
    },
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-DEBUG-AUTH-003",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "JTAG/SimDTM/DMI debug requester.",
      "operation": "Issue DMI read/write/debug operations and use debug module AXI master path.",
